item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Iconic

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that. +1

item.title: Re: Object Class
item.content:encoded:
First, I believe that this SCP was Euclid since 2008, so it is unlikely that it will change. However, I will response since similar SCPs might exist in the future, and the discussion might be relevant (although, the forums would probably be a better locations for this discussion, but I am not cleared where, how and if it should be brought up there).

Personally, I think it depend on whether the anomalous nature of SCP-004 is required to contain the stored SCPs.

  • If at least one of the SCPs must be stored outside the normal reality/dimension, then I think that you are correct and it should be Thaumiel. However, I didn't see any reference to that1.
  • If it is only used as a place that is both hard to reach and hard to spy on, than it won't be Thaumiel (in my opinion), as it is not actually used to contain those SCPs, just to store them . In this case, SCP-004 should probably be safe since active anomalous effects2 are present only when combining a key with either a (specific) door or a (specific) box3. Even if containment include the keys by themselves, it still should not be a problem.
Footnotes
1. While pages of other SCPs might reference SCP-004 as part of their containment procedures, that is irrelevant since continuity is not enforced cross-pages
2. Passive anomalous effect, such as indestructibly might be present, but won't change the classification as it does not effect the foundtion ability to contain any of SCP-004 elements
3. Making SCP-004 much easier to contain than SCP-005 (for example) that will effect any lock.

item.title: Object Class
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't this be Thaumiel-class, seeing as it not only contains other scps, but actually an entire site?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you want, you can ask a member of the Technical Team because tagging is one of their jobs. But I'm really not sure that this tag is necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Give it the wooden tag as it’s a wooden door

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The image has been sourced to here, a photograph by CinnamonCandy.

Licensing has come to the following conclusion:

The artist has not publicly stated *how* they want us to use their work - merely that, if we do use it, we credit and link it. Thus, we cannot assume they want us to use it under the terms of CC BY-SA 3.0.

The new image is by Roget and is licensed under CC BY-SA 3.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The juvenile suspects and Sheriff █████████████████ are terminated.

That's… uh… kinda dickish, guys.

item.title: Re: Timeline
item.content:encoded: Huge Necropost, but I think that the Foundation just lets researchers take whatever empty slot they want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Testing 005 on this door totally sounds like something doctor bright would do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am glad that the folks on here have changed it over the years. Honestly, I don't think it would be as well received as it is without them. I'm not a writer, by any means…so these guys made it what it is. Not me. I wrote the original during a lunch break while I was in college because I thought it would be neat to have an SCP that could contain other SCP's.

As far as the elder god goes…yeah it was essentially a nod to Cthulhu. I would be happy if that part was removed or turned into something more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
004, the door. And some keys.
This is also one of the older entries, and compared to the 2018 version of the previous entries, how does it look?
It looks alright.
Lots of the stuff here is a tad discordant, what with the elder god and the body teleportation and the time displacement and the box and the dreams of something, but at it’s core, it’s a pretty good scip. I am glad that it’s been edited over time, cutting chaff and nonsense details (“mind raped”), and it’s written in a way that doesn’t immediately make me feel lost, considering the amount of stuff it needs to get in.
That said, it has a few flaws. For one, a good deal of stuff could still be removed. The elder god is cool, but it would be nice if we got more info on him than “scary thing”, as well as a better connection to the rest of the effects.
Also, there’s the killing if civvies. I’m a lot more willing to forgive the deaths of a few secluded witnesses in comparison to 002, but this could still be fixed up, either by replacing it with amnestics or adding a note about someone acting out of bounds, or a lack of the resource, especially since it seems we can ask the author this time around.
I would also like to connect the box a bit more, because as it is, it’s kinda disconnected. Also, the stuff about dreaming is obviously a remnant to a link.
Lastly, I see nothing in the effects that would necessitate the faking of the death of the whole facility. I just don’t see it.
All in all, a slightly cheesy, but overall nice scip. I won’t upvote, since I think that the things that need a touchup are numerous, but it’s a nice idea that deserves to stay.
+0

Also, kudos to deus for actually checking out how his old scip is doing, and being chill with people changing it a bit. It’s cool to know that one of the old old (ancient?) guard is still around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is easily one of my favourite articles on the site. It's an old classic for me. Short as it is, it really gets to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yup

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We're screwed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
someone opens 004 with 005
they stretch across every single of the infinite possible universes in the multiverse
the multiverse collapses

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If this scip had any more blacked content I'd swear I was on PornHub.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …well, and also because long ago crosstests were often silly and unprofessional, and tended to make the Foundation look like idiots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The reason that cross testing is frowned upon is because some time ago that was used as a trick to seem better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Author decision, though a lot of it might be because this was originally written years and years ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is the site is located on not expunged or at least blanked?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Um, that comment was from 10 years ago…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: may I ask why there shouldn't be any connecting of SCPs? I think since they are all under the SCP Foundations control why wouldn't the researchers be trying different SCP's together? they are all anomalies so why not try to see if one anomaly might go with or react to another?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the same exact thing but have no clue how to go about it; do I need the okay from the original writers or what? I'm really wanting to start a "Testing" page where we could explore the testing of SCP's on/with other SCP's

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent edit was reverted, as it was an unauthorized edit. As per the Site Rules:

* Editing Other Articles: You may make minor edits for grammar or spelling to other peoples' articles. Please fill out the 'Short description of changes' box. Major edits require permission from the original author or staff. Stylistic and tonal edits are considered major edits.

Blackboxing content counts as a stylistic edit, and as such should not be done without permission from the original author or staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it is the Amnestic Use Guide?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would anyone know what this tale is? I can't find something with an alternate explanation for amnestics.

item.title: Bad Idea?
item.content:encoded: Considering items placed within the SCP-004-14 simply vanish without a trace instead of being destroyed, I wager that would be an ineffective method to dispose of SCP-682. The piece regarding that things placed within SCP-004-14 have a recorded affect on something's dreams is cause for concern. Imagine if said thing were able to project nightmares into reality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe someone could write about an experiment, trying to destroy SCP-682 by lowering its body mass to a small enough amount to fit into SCP-004-14. Close the box.

Looking back, this skip really illustrates what good SCPs should be. Just mysterious enough. Just enough horror. Visually appealing. The fact that the description is given in a 'chronological history' makes me sigh, but the anomaly itself is good enough to remedy that.

item.title: Re: Confirmation
item.content:encoded: Yes. This was one of the original SCP's before amnesiacs. We were a little trigger happy back then.

item.title: Confirmation
item.content:encoded: Just to confirm. The witnesses were killed after investigation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep, I originally posted this on /x/ on 4chan. I wrote this shortly after reading SCP-173 for the first time. At the time those silly "Holder" creepy pastas were all the rage, and most SCP's at the time had the agency killing everyone that knew too much.

The original version also had the other half of "Josie the Half Cat" existing in the pocket dimension where Site 62 resides.

So I say, if you guys have a good idea for updating the story to modern standards by removing all the needless terminations, go for it. Killing witnesses isn't what SCP-004 or any SCP is about anyways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I honestly am afraid to touch it. I feel like I would just mess it up if I attempted to revise it, as I have attempted to write two more SCP's and they were rejected immediately =P. I give my full blessing to anyone who wants to try to modernize it. If you wouldn't mind though, just run what you are thinking by me, and chances are I would approve it.

You are all much better writers than I am, I believe anything you guys could do would only make it better =). And hey…maybe we could chat and you could help me fix one of my recent SCP's to see if we can make it something worth putting up on the wiki again?

item.title: Re: Very unusual
item.content:encoded: Thanks =). It was the one good thing I had ever written. I doubt I will ever replicate that success again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I first wrote SCP-004 I was on an HP Lovecraft kick, so yeah, you guessed right. It was meant to represent some sort of sleeping elder god, a nod to Lovecraft.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just can't help but think of Cthulhu and his spawns.

item.title: Very unusual
item.content:encoded:
I like how this SCP is both safe and dangerous at the same time. It is slightly confusing as well. I had to reread it twice to make sure I understood which keys were being used.

Now this is an unusually calm SCP. A displacement of reality, possibly opening into a dimension where a large storage facility actually exists in another realm of reality. as well, the other keys may open doors to places where earth does not exist at all, this explaining why the persons that open the doors are torn apart like that. In that realm, earth does not exist, hence, they do not exist. Their remains displayed by the time dilation effect.

To that point, the effect that time inside the SCP moves at a different pace is very nice. In essence, one could survive a year in there, but much more has passed out here. I like this thought.

Definate upvote!!

item.title: Mod Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Noting that a recent edit was reverted, as it made a substantial content change without author permission. Editors, if you believe that an article's object class should be changed, please contact the original writer(s) and obtain permission for the change first.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It will stay as it is unless the original author decides to revise it, or the rating drops into the negatives. It looks like the original author was deusprogrammer who hasn't been active for two years. The article itself is sitting on a plus four hundred and something rating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looking back through the comments, I'm seeing a lot of the same stuff - the termination of the witnesses doesn't jive with more modern portrayals the Foundation. I know it's because it's an older piece, so I think it's time to take a good hard look at it and determine whether it should stay as is, needs revision, or doesn't mesh well anymore. Personally, I think it's a matter of needing revision. Any thoughts?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What would happen if SCP-005 is used to open the door?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ph'nglui mglw'nafh Cthulhu R'lyeh wgah'nagl fhtagn!

+1
CLAP CLAP CLAP CLAP CLAP CLAP CLAP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out-of-universe, I'm pretty sure this was written before the concept of amnestics was developed. SCP-002 has the same problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The witness killing is really the only thing that bugs me about 004.

The amnestics (or lack thereof) excuse wouldn't work, imo, as there are more than a few other entries using them earlier than the 40s. Yeah, yeah, no canon, but really there is, to some degree.

It doesn't stand out as much in this one, being just three people, compared to others, like 002 and the 1/3 of a village being murdered in order to intimidate the rest into not talking… or something…?

Anyway, I still gave it a +1 (which I for some reason didn't do before… maybe I read this one before I actually joined the site? *shrug*) because everything else is so freaking good about it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alternative timelines I guess.
Edit:Godamnit, Necro'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 6.84477 years and still no one has done this. Seems like a fun idea to write about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it didn't list the SCP as being in North America, I'd have said it's in one of the Soviet Bloc countries and the GRU did their usual cleanup.

Now now, I'm pretty sure the US was just as trigger-happy as the Soviet Block during late 40s, if not more so.

I agree with that part being unnecessary, but I'm accepting it due to the SCP being old. It doesn't ruin the article either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if I' necroing, but the information around SCP-004-05 would definitely be Thaumiel; I can even imagine the whole sections about -05 being redacted, and the Foundation acting as if there are really only 11 keys, with the fifth key being under a "Warning: Level 5 or 4 Clearance Needed" tab

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, somewhere there is a story about how amnestics work. It's… not what you think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess those are pretty plausible reasons, and now that I think about it, it would make sense if at least some agents, doctors, and containment teams were a little more brutal than others. Even though they are all supposed to have the same professional integrity, people are all different.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the ideas is that the Foundation occasionally absorbs other groups. If it didn't list the SCP as being in North America, I'd have said it's in one of the Soviet Bloc countries and the GRU did their usual cleanup. Maybe some "Anti-Red" group was in charge.

Or it may have been before amnestics were available.

Or it could have been someone exceeding their authority - or the released cover story already listed them as dead for some reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really love the attention to detail and the amount of details at that. However I do find the termination of the sheriff and the kids who originally found SCP-004 to be a little unrealistic and unnecessary. Why not administer amnestics to them instead? Especially because I'm sure those people would be missed if they went missing or died under suspicious circumstances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is basically a Thaumiel class, isn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I first wrote this, I was on an HP Lovecraft kick, so the giant green monster was originally Cthulhu. It would probably serve the SCP better if we removed it, or perhaps expand on it a bit and make it more interesting.

Also, originally, my idea was that the effects of the door weren't apparent until crossing the threshold of the door. In fact once you pass even partially through the door frame and you are using one of the unsafe keys, you were sucked in and torn asunder in a variety of ways.

Otherwise, I would find it interesting to explore the effects of the objects passed into the unsafe room on this plane of existence. Of course as some of them are passages to other universes completely separate and different from our own, they may never show effects on our world/timeline. Figuring out which doors have an effect on ours would be interesting though.

Another thing I wondered after writing this…why doesn't SCP use inanimate objects to test with, before using death row inmates? I guess a condemned criminal is a cheaper sacrifice than a baseball.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some details about how the planes found in SCP-004-2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 10, 11 and 13 interact with our plane of reality would be cool. Are we unable to perceive anything beyond the doorway? Does the difference in cosmic music or whatever cause the space around the door to blur when it's open? As is, all we know is that opening the door causes people to come apart and vanish, which would imply that the doorway doesn't stop whatever the difference is from coming through; is this having any negative effects on the fabric of our own plane of reality?

The article's quality isn't really in question; I found it immensely creepy, although the giant green monster didn't really do anything for me. It just also left me with a bunch of dumb mechanical questions.

item.title: Re: Timeline
item.content:encoded: That's not how it works. A lot of SCPs have letters instead of numbers, and the numbers are arbitrary. SCP-1000 was discovered by a Foundation precursor in the 1400s, for example.

item.title: Timeline
item.content:encoded:
Nearly everything I've seen shows the Foundation to be around a century old. This SCP was discovered 65 years ago. I can imagine what life must have been like.

Agent [REDACTED]: I remember the last time when we discovered an SCP. It was nearly 20 years ago now when my task force found 003.

Agent [DATA EXPUNGED]: Really? Tell me about it! (Agent [REDACTED] tells Agent [DATA EXPUNGED] how SCP-003 was obtained)

Agent [DATA EXPUNGED]: Wow! I wish I could discover an SCP someday.
